title: Your First 30 Minutes
summary: A reliable opening route for stabilizing a fresh character before traveling inland.

steps.title: Check your starting condition
steps.body: Identify your food, water, temperature, blood, and health status before sprinting away. Avoid wasting energy when your food and hydration are already low.

steps.title: Orient yourself
steps.body: Use the coastline, road signs, railway lines, major terrain features, and the direction of the sun to estimate your position. DZ-Atlas can confirm the town once you find a sign.

steps.title: Find a cutting tool
steps.body: Prioritize a knife or any tool that can cut. A cutting tool unlocks rags, bark, kindling, animal preparation, and many early survival actions.

steps.title: Secure clean water
steps.body: Drink from a water pump when possible. Empty suspicious containers before refilling them with known clean water.

steps.title: Collect basic food
steps.body: Search houses, greenhouses, small shops, infected, fruit trees, boats, and nearby utility buildings. Eat enough to stabilize, but keep moving.

steps.title: Build a basic medical reserve
steps.body: Carry disinfected bandages or clean rags and keep them accessible. One additional bandage can save a run after multiple cuts.

steps.title: Leave the coast deliberately
steps.body: Once stable, choose a destination with a reason: a clinic, police station, hunting area, military route, or meeting point.

tips: Jogging usually preserves more energy than sprinting everywhere.
Listen before entering buildings; infected and players often reveal themselves through sound.
A quiet town is not necessarily an empty town.
warnings: Drinking untreated surface water can cause illness.
Do not eat with bloody hands after preparing an animal.
Fresh-spawn areas are often more dangerous because players repeatedly pass through them.

verification.note: Designed for vanilla DayZ. Exact values and recipes should be verified against the current game files before publication.

presentation.coverAlt: DayZ scene illustrating Your First 30 Minutes

reader.completionMessage: You are ready to put this guide into practice.
